content.question: "Not until she ___ the results did she realize the gravity of the situation."

explanation: Inverted structure with 'Not until' + Past Perfect for the earlier action.

content.question: "I ___ here since 2019."

explanation: Present Perfect with 'since' — started in the past, continues to now.

content.question: "This car needs ___ before winter arrives."

explanation: 'Need + gerund' = znaczenie bierne (coś trzeba z czymś zrobić) — 'needs servicing' = 'needs to be serviced'.

content.question: "Look! The children ___ in the park."

explanation: 'Look!' signals Present Continuous — action happening now.

content.question: "This time next week, I ___ on a beach in Greece."

explanation: Future Continuous: action in progress at a specific future time.

content.question: "By the time we arrived, the film ___."

explanation: Past Perfect: action completed before another past action.

content.question: "I ___ play football every weekend when I was a child, but now I prefer cycling."

explanation: 'Used to + bezokolicznik' — zwyczaje w przeszłości, których już nie wykonujemy.

content.question: "While I ___ dinner, the phone rang."

explanation: Past Continuous (tło) + Past Simple (zdarzenie nagłe) — klasyczna narracja przeszła.

content.question: "I've known Mark ___ we were at primary school together."

explanation: 'Since' + konkretny moment początkowy (we were at school). 'For' + długość okresu.

content.question: Choose the correct form: "She ___ to school every day."

explanation: Present Simple, 3rd person singular → goes.

content.question: "Look at those dark clouds! It ___ rain any moment now."

explanation: 'Be going to' — przewidywanie na podstawie widocznych dowodów (dark clouds).

content.question: "Rarely ___ such a talented young musician in recent years."

explanation: Inwersja po wyrażeniu negatywnym 'Rarely': Rarely + have/had + podmiot + V3.

content.question: "I wish I ___ harder for the exam last week."

explanation: Wish + Past Perfect for regrets about the past.

content.question: "She ___ three novels by the end of this year."

explanation: Future Perfect: completed action before a future deadline.

content.question: "By the end of next month, I ___ in this company for ten years."

explanation: Future Perfect — czynność zakończona do konkretnego punktu w przyszłości ('by the end of').

content.question: "Every summer as a child, we ___ visit my grandparents in the countryside."

explanation: 'Would + V' opisuje powtarzające się czynności w przeszłości (obok 'used to'). Różnica: 'would' NIE pasuje do stanów.

content.question: "Have you ___ finished your homework?" "No, not ___."

explanation: Already — pytania 'czy już?'. Yet — odpowiedzi 'jeszcze nie'. Klasyczne połączenie Present Perfect.

content.question: "I ___ to Spain three times in my life — the last time ___ in 2022."

explanation: Doświadczenie życiowe = Present Perfect ('have been'). Konkretny moment (2022) = Past Simple ('was').

content.question: "She ___ that he is telling the truth." (state verb — believe)

explanation: 'Believe' to czasownik stanu — nie używamy go w czasach continuous. Present Simple, 3rd person → believes.

content.question: "Keep quiet! The baby ___."

explanation: Present Continuous — czynność w trakcie trwania w chwili mówienia.

content.question: "My feet were sore because I ___ all day in the mountains."

explanation: Past Perfect Continuous — czynność trwająca w przeszłości, której skutki widać w innym punkcie przeszłości (sore feet).

content.question: "Hardly ___ the door when the phone rang."

explanation: Inversion after 'Hardly': Hardly + had + subject + V3.

content.question: "By the time he retires, he ___ for this company for 40 years."

explanation: Future Perfect Continuous: duration up to a future point.

content.question: "The baby ___ for over an hour now — something must be wrong."

explanation: Present Perfect Continuous — czynność trwająca od przeszłości do teraz, 'for over an hour now'.

content.question: "What ___ when I called you yesterday evening?"

explanation: Past Continuous — czynność w trakcie trwania w konkretnym momencie przeszłości.

content.question: "Don't call at 8 PM — I ___ dinner with my in-laws."

explanation: Future Continuous — czynność trwająca w konkretnym momencie w przyszłości (at 8 PM).

content.question: "It's high time you ___ looking for a proper job."

explanation: 'It's (high/about) time + Past Simple' — konstrukcja wyrażająca, że czas najwyższy coś zrobić (znaczenie teraźniejsze/przyszłe, forma przeszła).

content.question: "Never in my life ___ such a breathtaking view."

explanation: Inwersja po wyrażeniu negatywnym 'Never in my life': Never + have/had + podmiot + V3.

options.text: "She is always complaining about her colleagues." (irritating habit)
"I am having a sister who lives in Canada."
"How long have you been learning English?"
"The sun rises in the east." (general truth)

content.question: Które zdania są GRAMATYCZNIE poprawne?

explanation: A: Present Cont. z 'always' dla irytujących zwyczajów ✓. B: 'have' (posiadać) jest state verb — 'I have a sister'. C: Present Perfect Cont. ✓. D: Present Simple — ogólna prawda ✓.

options.text: "I am liking this song very much."
"She had been studying when the power went out."

content.question: Które zdania są gramatycznie POPRAWNE?

explanation: A: Present Simple dla rozkładów ✓. B: 'like' to state verb — 'I like'. C: Past Perfect Cont. ✓. D: 'since Monday' wymaga Present Perfect → 'Have you seen'.

options.text: "I'm seeing the dentist at 3 PM tomorrow." (appointment)
"She has been to Paris twice." (life experience)

content.question: Which sentences are correct?

explanation: A: Present Continuous for arrangements ✓. B: should be "boils" (general fact → Present Simple). C: Present Perfect for experience ✓. D: "know" is a state verb — no continuous.

options.text: "They have been waiting for over an hour."

content.question: Które zdania są poprawne?

explanation: A: Present Perfect + since ✓. B: powinno być 'has been working' lub 'has worked'. C: Present Perfect Cont. ✓. D: Present Simple dla rozkładów ✓.

options.text: "I leave for Rome on Friday." (plan ticketu)
"She is to meet the president tomorrow." (formal schedule)
"We are about to start — stay quiet!"

content.question: Które zdania NATURALNIE wyrażają przyszłość?

explanation: Present Simple (rozkład) ✓. 'Be to + V' (formalny plan) ✓. 'Be about to' (zaraz, za chwilę) ✓. D: 'is commencing yesterday' = sprzeczność czasowa.

content.question:
Przepisz z użyciem FOR: "They met in 2010. They are still best friends."
They ___.
content.sampleAnswer: They have been best friends for over 15 years.

explanation: Spotkanie w przeszłości + nadal prawdziwe → Present Perfect (Continuous) + 'for'.

content.question:
Przepisz używając Past Perfect Continuous: "Her eyes were red. She had been crying for hours."
The reason her eyes were red was that ___.
content.sampleAnswer: The reason her eyes were red was that she had been crying for hours.

explanation: Past Perfect Continuous — długotrwała czynność w przeszłości, której skutki widać.

content.question:
Przepisz używając Past Perfect: "I finished my homework. Then I watched TV."
When I ___.
content.sampleAnswer: When I had finished my homework, I watched TV.

explanation: Past Perfect wyraża czynność ZAKOŃCZONĄ przed inną w przeszłości.

explanation: Past Perfect Continuous/Simple + Past Continuous (tło) + Future Continuous (moment w przyszłości).

explanation: 4 aspekty × 3 strefy czasowe = 12 czasów angielskich.

explanation: Have you ever been to Japan?

content.question: Oceń poprawność zdań.

statements.text: "Did you ever visit Rome?" — poprawne pytanie w Present Perfect.
"She's been to Paris twice this year." — Present Perfect dla doświadczeń życiowych.

explanation: 'Did you ever visit' to Past Simple — powinno być 'Have you ever visited'. Present Perfect z 'this year' ✓.

content.question: Decide if each sentence is grammatically correct.

statements.text: "I have been waiting for two hours." — Present Perfect Continuous.
"She has went to the shop." — correct Past Participle.

explanation: Present Perfect Continuous ✓. 'went' is Past Simple; Past Participle = 'gone'.

content.question: Oceń poprawność zdań dotyczących czasów.

statements.text: "I'm seeing the doctor at 10 tomorrow." (arranged meeting)
"I see your point." — poprawne użycie 'see' jako state verb.
"She has left for Paris last week." — poprawne Present Perfect.
"By 2040, we will be having solved climate change." — poprawny Future Perfect Continuous.

explanation: 'See' ma dwa znaczenia: dynamic (spotkać się — continuous OK) + state (rozumieć). Last week + Present Perfect = błąd. Future Perfect: 'will have solved' (nie continuous).

content.question: Oceń poprawność.

statements.text: "I've been to Madrid twice this year." — Present Perfect z 'this year' = poprawne.
"When did you last see him?" — poprawna forma pytania o ostatni raz.
"She has left yesterday morning." — poprawne użycie Present Perfect z konkretnym czasem.
"By the time the show starts, we will have taken our seats." — poprawny Future Perfect.

explanation: Twice this year ✓. When did you last ✓. Present Perfect NIE łączy się z 'yesterday' (konkretny czas = Past Simple). Future Perfect ✓.

content.question: Oceń poprawność zdań (czasy).

statements.text: "I am knowing her for ages." — poprawne użycie Present Continuous.
"She's always losing her keys." — Present Continuous z 'always' wyraża irytującą rutynę.
"They have been married for 20 years." — Present Perfect dla stanu trwającego do teraz.
"Did you ever eat sushi?" — poprawne pytanie o doświadczenie życiowe.

explanation: 'Know' to state verb — Present Perfect. 'Always + Continuous' ✓. Present Perfect dla trwającego stanu ✓. Doświadczenie życiowe → 'Have you ever eaten'.

steps.text: Last summer I went to Italy with my family.
We have stayed in a small hotel near the beach.
Every morning we had breakfast on the terrace.
It was the best holiday I had ever had.

content.question: A student wrote about their holiday. Find the step with a tense error.

explanation: "We have stayed" — should be "We stayed" (finished past action with "last summer").

steps.text: Last weekend we went hiking in the Tatras.
It was my third visit this year.
We have stayed at a small hostel near Zakopane.
On Sunday morning, we watched a beautiful sunrise from the mountain peak.

content.question: Znajdź błąd w krótkiej relacji.

explanation: #3: 'have stayed' — Present Perfect nie pasuje do zakończonej przeszłości (last weekend). Powinno być 'We stayed'.

steps.text: I was walking home from work when I heard a strange noise.
I turned around but didn't see anything.
Suddenly, a black cat has jumped in front of me.
It stared at me for a moment and then ran away.

content.question: Znajdź zdanie z błędem w poniższej krótkiej opowieści.

explanation: Zdanie 3: 'has jumped' — w narracji przeszłej nie używamy Present Perfect. Powinno być 'jumped'.

steps.text: I'll never forget the day I met my husband.
It was a rainy Tuesday in October, and I was running late for a job interview.
As I was entering the café, I bumped into a tall man who had held an umbrella.
By the end of our chat, I had completely forgotten about the interview.

content.question: Znajdź błąd czasu w opowiadaniu.

explanation: #3: 'who had held' → 'who was holding' (czynność równoległa, Past Continuous), nie Past Perfect.

steps.text: It was a beautiful Saturday morning.
I was drinking my coffee when I heard a knock at the door.
I opened it and saw my neighbour, who has been crying.
She told me her cat had run away the night before.

content.question: Znajdź krok z błędem czasu.

explanation: W narracji przeszłej używamy Past Perfect Continuous: 'who had been crying', nie Present Perfect Continuous.

content.context:
Read the text:
"Tom has lived in Warsaw for five years. Before that, he was living in Kraków, where he had spent his childhood. Next year, he is moving to Berlin because he has found a new job there."

subQuestions.text: How long has Tom lived in Warsaw?
Where did Tom spend his childhood?
Assess the statements.

statements.text: "is moving" expresses a planned future arrangement.
"has found" is Past Simple.

explanation: 5 years ✓. Kraków ✓. 'is moving' = arrangement. 'has found' = Present Perfect.

content.template: Last night when I {{v1}} (get) home, my flatmate {{v2}} (already / cook) dinner. While we {{v3}} (eat), we heard a strange noise from the corridor.

explanation: Past Simple (got) + Past Perfect (wcześniejsza czynność) + Past Continuous (tło dla przerwania).

content.template: When I {{v1}} (arrive) at the cinema, the film {{v2}} (already / start). I {{v3}} (miss) the beginning completely.

explanation: Past Simple + Past Perfect (wcześniejsza czynność) + Past Simple (konsekwencja).

content.template: Yesterday, while I {{v1}} (walk) home, I {{v2}} (see) a fox. It {{v3}} (stand) in the middle of the road.
content.instruction: Fill in the correct tense form.

explanation: Narrative past: Past Continuous (background) + Past Simple (sudden event) + Past Continuous (description).

content.template: My brother {{v1}} (live) in Berlin since 2020. Before that, he {{v2}} (work) for a company in London for five years. Next summer, he {{v3}} (move) back to Poland.

explanation: Present Perfect (since 2020) + Past Perfect (wcześniejszy okres w przeszłości) + Present Cont. (zaplanowana przyszłość).

content.template: Don't phone me between 7 and 8 — I {{v1}} (have) dinner with my family. At 8:30 we {{v2}} (probably / finish) and I'll be free. I {{v3}} (call) you back then.

explanation: Future Continuous (trwanie w momencie) + Future Perfect (ukończenie przed momentem) + Future Simple (decyzja).

content.question: "If only I ___ what she meant by that comment."

explanation: 'If only + Past Simple' — żal dotyczący teraźniejszości (= I wish I knew).

content.question: "I'll call you ___ I get home."

explanation: 'As soon as' + Present Simple dla przyszłości. 'Until' ma przeciwny kierunek czasowy.

content.question: "You can borrow the car ___ you fill it up before you return it."

explanation: 'As long as' = pod warunkiem że (= provided that). 'Unless' = jeśli nie. 'In case' = na wypadek.

content.question: "If you heat ice, it ___ into water." (physical fact)

explanation: Zero Conditional: If + Present Simple, Present Simple — prawdy ogólne, prawa fizyki.

content.question: "Take your umbrella ___ it starts raining."

explanation: 'In case' = na wypadek gdyby (środek ostrożności). 'Unless' = jeśli nie.

content.question: "If I ___ more responsible in my twenties, I wouldn't be struggling financially now."

explanation: Mixed Conditional: Past Perfect (przyczyna w przeszłości) + would + bezokolicznik (skutek teraz).

content.question: "___ I to win the lottery tomorrow, I would donate half to charity."

explanation: Inwersja w Second Conditional: 'Were + podmiot + to + V' = 'If I were to win'.

content.question: "If you ___ to the warning, you wouldn't be in this mess now."

explanation: Mixed Conditional: Past Perfect (przyczyna w przeszłości) + would + V (teraźniejszy skutek).

content.question: "If the weather ___ nice tomorrow, we'll go to the beach."

explanation: First Conditional: If + Present Simple, will + V. Po 'if' nie używamy 'will'.

content.question: "___ she have finished her degree, she would have landed a better job."

explanation: Inwersja w Third Conditional: 'Had she finished' = 'If she had finished'.

content.question: "If it rains tomorrow, we ___ at home."

explanation: First Conditional: If + Present Simple, will + base form.

content.question: "If I ___ you, I would apologize."

explanation: Second Conditional: If + Past Simple. "were" is used for all persons in formal English.

content.question: "But for your help, I ___ the project on time."

explanation: 'But for' = 'If it hadn't been for'. Third Conditional equivalent.

content.question: "Unless you hurry, you ___ the bus."

explanation: Unless = If...not. First Conditional pattern.

content.question: "If she ___ the scholarship, she wouldn't be struggling with debt now."

explanation: Mixed Conditional: If + Past Perfect (past cause), would + base (present result).

content.question: "If she had studied harder, she ___ the exam."

explanation: Third Conditional: If + Past Perfect, would have + V3.

content.question: "___ I known about the meeting, I would have attended."

explanation: Inverted Third Conditional: Had + subject + V3 (= If I had known).

content.question: "I wish you ___ stop tapping your pen — it's driving me crazy!"

explanation: 'Wish + would' — irytacja zachowaniem kogoś, którego chciałbyś zmienić.

content.question: Które wyrażenia mogą zastąpić 'if' w zdaniach warunkowych?

explanation: Provided that / supposing / assuming = alternatywy 'if'. 'Even though' = kontrast, nie warunek.

content.question:
Przepisz w Zero Conditional: "Water boils when heated to 100°C."
If you ___.
content.sampleAnswer: If you heat water to 100°C, it boils.

explanation: Zero Conditional: If + Present Simple, Present Simple — uniwersalne prawdy.

content.rubric: Inwersja 'Had it not been for' (1), sens (1).
content.question:
Przepisz używając HAD IT NOT BEEN FOR: "If my parents hadn't supported me, I wouldn't have finished university."
Had ___.
content.sampleAnswer: Had it not been for my parents' support, I wouldn't have finished university.

explanation: 'If it hadn't been for X' → 'Had it not been for X'. Inwersja formalna, synonimy: 'But for'.

content.question:
Napisz Second Conditional: "I'm not a millionaire, so I can't buy an island."
If I ___.
content.sampleAnswer: If I were/was a millionaire, I could/would buy an island.

explanation: Second Cond. hipoteza o teraźniejszości: If + Past Simple, would/could + V.

content.question:
Przepisz zdanie używając First Conditional: "Hurry up or you'll miss the bus."
If ___.
content.sampleAnswer: If you don't hurry up, you'll miss the bus.

explanation: First Conditional: If + Present Simple (negative), will + bezokolicznik.

content.rubric: Past Perfect + would have V3 (1), sens (1).
content.question:
Napisz Third Conditional: "She was careless, so she lost her passport."
If she ___.
content.sampleAnswer: If she hadn't been careless, she wouldn't have lost her passport.

explanation: Third Cond. dla przeszłych hipotez: If + Past Perfect, would(n't) have + V3.

content.question: Rewrite using a Third Conditional: 'I didn't study, so I failed the exam.'
content.sampleAnswer: If I had studied, I wouldn't have failed the exam.

explanation: If + Past Perfect, would(n't) + have + V3.

content.question: Match the conditional type with its use.

explanation: 0: always true. 1: possible future. 2: unreal present. 3: unreal past.

"Suppose you won a million pounds" — wprowadza sytuację hipotetyczną.

explanation: 'If I was' akceptowalne potocznie (formalnie: 'were'). 'Suppose/What if' to alternatywy dla 'If' w hipotezach.

"Provided that you study, you will pass." — "provided that" can replace "if".

explanation: Second Cond.: 'If I had more time' (NOT 'would have'). 'Provided that' = if ✓.

content.question: Oceń zdania warunkowe.

statements.text: "Supposing you won the lottery, what would you do?" — 'Supposing' zastępuje 'if' w Second Cond.
"If I will have time, I'll call you." — poprawne użycie Future w klauzuli 'if'.

explanation: Supposing/imagine/suppose — alternatywy 'if' ✓. 'If I will' — błąd (Present Simple po if). Had/Were — inwersje formalne ✓.

content.question: Oceń struktury warunkowe.

statements.text: "But for your warning, I would have walked into a trap." — poprawna konstrukcja.
"If I hadn't been so tired, I would go to the party last night." — prawidłowy mixed conditional.
"Were it not for the rain, we would be at the beach now." — formalny mixed conditional.
"I'll help you if you need." — poprawne uzupełnione First Cond.

explanation: But for ✓. 'would go... last night' powinno być 'would have gone' (Third Cond.). Were it not for ✓. 'If you need' — brak dopełnienia (if you need it / need help).

content.template: If I {{v1}} (be) you, I'd tell her the truth. Unless it {{v2}} (stop) raining soon, we'll cancel the picnic. If she hadn't missed the bus, she {{v3}} (be) here by now.

explanation: 2nd Cond. (were) + 1st Cond. z 'unless' (Present Simple) + Mixed Cond. (past → present result).

content.template: If I {{v1}} (can) speak Spanish, I'd apply for that job in Madrid. I wish my boss {{v2}} (stop) calling me on weekends — it's so annoying. If we had left earlier, we {{v3}} (not / get stuck) in traffic.

content.template: If she {{v1}} (not miss) her flight, she would be at the conference right now. But for the traffic jam, she {{v2}} (catch) it. Next time, she {{v3}} (leave) earlier.

explanation: Mixed Cond. (past → present result) + Third Cond. (but for = if it hadn't been for) + First Cond.

content.template: If I {{v1}} (listen) to my mum's advice back then, I wouldn't be in debt now. Should you need any help, just {{v2}} (call) me. Had they left earlier, they {{v3}} (not miss) the flight.

content.template: If I {{v1}} (be) taller, I would play basketball. If I had left earlier, I {{v2}} (not miss) the train.

explanation: 2nd: were/was. 3rd: wouldn't have missed.

content.question: Oceń poprawność.

statements.text: "The bridge is expected to be opened next month." — poprawna forma przyszła bierna.
"She asked me when am I leaving." — poprawny szyk pytania zależnego.
"I had my car serviced yesterday." — poprawna konstrukcja causative.
"The report was written by he." — poprawna forma zaimka po 'by'.

content.question: Oceń poprawność transformacji/reported.

statements.text: Active: "He gave me the book." → Passive 1: "I was given the book." + Passive 2: "The book was given to me." — obie poprawne.
"She said, 'Don't go there.'" → Reported: "She told not go there."
"He admitted stealing the car." — poprawne: admit + -ing.
Active: "Phrasal verbs can be used in passive." — 'are looked after' jest poprawną formą.

explanation: Ditransitive: dwie formy bierne ✓. Reported imperative: 'told me not to go'. Admit + -ing ✓. Passive + phrasal ✓.

"He said me that he was tired." — poprawna konstrukcja z czasownikiem 'say'.

Reported: "He told that he was tired." — poprawna struktura z 'tell'.

explanation: A bridge is being built ✓. 'A book was given TO him' lub 'He was given a book'. Will → would ✓. 'Tell' wymaga odpowiednika: 'He told me that...'.
